mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over 6 months indicates rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a tightening monetary cycle and labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 1% and UNRATE falls < 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decline in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over 6 months, combined with a 0.5% increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over the same period, signals a weakening demand and labor market, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL falls < 1% and UNRATE rises > 0.5% in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in low-demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months, combined with a 0.5% decline in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals a weakening labor market and demand, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 1% and CPIAUCSL falls < 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decline in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over 6 months, combined with a 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the same period, signals a weakening supply and monetary cycle, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls < 2% and FEDFUNDS rises > 1% in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in low-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
